top_profane.text:
I have been a loyal PC user since 1993. I was the guy who thumbed my nose at apple users. To me they were the tree hugger, vegan, politically correct idiots of the computer world. No way would I ever be caught with an apple. PC's had way more software available, were way cheaper, and were way more flexible. Fast forward 22 years. I've been converted to the tree huger computer club. I have already switched from an android phone to an IPhone 6plus, and purchased an IPad air 2. I will soon spend $3000 + on a IMac. Good by PC and kiss my a..

I had a small degree of hope that W10 would make my pc experience more pleasant and less of an absurd search and destroy mission to try and make my computer regain some degree of speed, stop crashing, quit doing random turrets syndrome like executions, and protect it against the seemingly unstoppable viruses. All hope is now gone.

Below is a list of my complaints and reasons for switching to apple:

1. Windows 8 really sucked big time. Even though windows 10 is better Microsoft still screwed over PC users by developing a OS for tablets and marketing it for PC's. I've had windows 8 since it was released. After all this time you would think you would get used to and be able to get around on your computer without having to think it through every freaking day!!! 10 is better but not much. It's just not user friendly or simple. How freaking hard is it to make something that makes sense Microsoft.. It's like you over think everything. I've had my IPad for 2 months now and navigating the internet makes sense and is so simple.

2. Viruses and malware: It seems like over the last 2 years that viruses and malware have become unstoppable. I have two programs that are rated the best to keep my PC clean. No such luck. I am constantly being plagued with crap on my computer that causes it to crash and sputter at the speed of a 286. It's driving me so crazy that I almost believe it's a Microsoft plot to make money off of virus protection software, and I detest conspiracy theorists. What in the heck is up Microsoft. Can you not get a handle on the virus/malware problem... answer no... you are the problem... it's promoted and profited off of by you... I'm freaking gone you twits. Hello Apple

3. It takes an expert to solve the mentioned problems: I know many of you are saying...well just do this and that and the problems are solved...first of all you literally have to be a PC expert to know what to do to clean up your computer... it's not just a matter of running Avast of something. Second I have no interest in taking the time to learn how to be an expert... I'm not in the biz and don't want to be.. I just want to be a home computer user and that's it. Screw all this scouring the internet to find the answer for an little bug or something. I'm not saying Apple has no problems or is perfect... but they sure seem to have a lot less problems. Goodbye Microsoft. You'll never see me again or my money... and believe me you've seen a lot of my money.

4. I HAVE MATURED: Although many of you are no doubt questioning my maturity if not my sanity after this rant believe it or not I have matured. My reasons for staying away from apple no longer exist. I don't totally disagree with tree huggers though they are a bit extreme(then again so is cutting down ever single tree bigger than a twig). As I get older I see the point of vegans, and people that advise a healthy diet. I will never be vegan, but just like a PC, crap in and you get crap results. Finally as far as politically correct. Well, see that's where I've always been mature so the rest of you need to convert in that area.

So hello my new liberal friends. I hope you will welcome me with open arms. In closing I would like to take one more jab at Microsoft. Thanks for nothing you idiots.
top_profane.title: IT'S TIME FOR AN APPLE

This is an update (the second paragraph is my original review). After several weeks with this new version, I see how inferior it is. I wonder if Microsoft is just blind or did they have to work at creating a product that is inferior to the older versions? It is so bad, I am condsidering eating the money I spent and getting the 2007 version. This is true of Outlook and Word, both of which I started with in 97 (I started with DOS in 87. Some idiot created this stupid ribbon, which is worthless. Most everything takes an extra step. Efficiency goes down. And in Outlook, I can't even use signatures like I use to. It won't allow it. I suggest people start migrating to Macs (I am considering it now), as Microsoft has been going downhill and they don't care what the consumer thinks. We need a company to create a better email client that was as good as the old Outlook and word processing that is as good as 2003. I actually paid to get an inferior product I could go on and on about all the reasons it's inferior, but I don't have time. DO NOT UPGRADE UNLESS YOU HAVE NO CHOICE. KEEP 2003 TILL THE END. THINK OF MOVING TO MAC. COMPLAIN TO MICROSOFT. Maybe it is because Gates is no longer there, but Microsoft doesn't have a clue about user friendly. Idiots are designing this new crap. I know. I've dealt with them since 1987. they improved till the mid 2000s. Vista must have been created by the same people who created the newest Office. Idiots. Windows 7 had some improvements, but just a few. But I don't see one improvement in the new Word or Outlook. They are worse than 2003 versions. Crap.

Outlook is okay, but it's layout is pure crap. There's no contrast to speak of like in the old OUtlook, so you can't easily see commands. My computer needs some serious fixing. When I saw this software being offered I was skeptical but figured with a PC as messed up as mine, what have I got to loose?

To my surprise you can only use this on up to 5 computers. You get to use it for one year. I did not think this would have limitations on it like that.

After installing the software Fix-It said it found many problems. One was even a surprise to me. It says I have McAfee installed. That simply shouldn't be. I looked in add/remove programs & don't see it there. I don't like McAfee & don't want it on my PC. I use other services for my antivirus. Fix-It even comes with anti-virus software! At first I thought that was pretty great. Since I couldn't uninstall the hidden McAfee & Fix-It didn't offer an uninstall option, I could not use Fix-It's anti-virus.

The only good thing this software did for my PC is resolve my insanely long shut down. So much crap is running & I don't know why. Shut down takes several minutes. When Fix-It was running it shut down quickly & normally. However that is the ONLY good service I am aware it provided me. Shutting down Fix-It also shuts down this repair so it is total doo doo.

The rest of Fix-It made my PC a world of confusion. My internet went down to a crawl. The PC kept freezing. Granted it is already doing this crap on a regular basis - now it's 10x's worse! Firefox wont load my usual websites but will load others okay. I had to open Internet Explorer to access the websites that wouldn't load on Firefox. Why? I don't know why. I wish I knew why! In the beginning, I did not associate this oddity with Fix-It. On my next computer start-up when the same wacky crap was going on I decided to close Fix-It. Ta Da! Everything ran normally, that is the crappy way it ran before Fix-It.

This is such a disappointment for me. It looks like I really have no choice & will have to do a reformat to get my PC cleaned up & running smoothly like before.

Based on my experience, I cannot recommend this product. If you are having PC problems, why make them worse? Avoid this one.
top_profane.title: Made My Computer Worse

top_profane.text:
This is a reasonable product let down by moronic failings. it is so frustrating, it's fit to be ripped off my PC. Here are a few of the crazy stupid things:

1. you can schedule the backup tool, but IT WON'T RUN at the schedule time. Instead it waits for you to acknowledge the previous AV scan first. This means (duh!) that that backup runs during the working day, ie when you get a chance to acknowledge the scan. Moronic!
2. the scan picks up tracking cookies every time and states these need to be dealt with BUT OFFERS NO WAY OF DEALING WITH THEM. Moronic!
3. there is no way to schedule the temporary files cleanup - so your PC is stated as "being at risk" until you manually do this. It this total rubbish! Of course my PC is NOT at risk just because I have a few days of temp files hanging around... and how about some way to schedule the cleanup? Moronic!
4. the backup function can be cancelled in the first 30 seconds, but as long as it STARTS this moronic product thinks you are protected. Of course, it should wait until the backup is FINISHED SUCCESSFULLY before flagging you as green. Moronic!
5. It fails to integrate with the Windows Security Console correctly - my machine first states I am green (ie OK) because I have a firewall running... then I drill in and it complains that I have TWO firewalls running... then I drill in further and it complains that the Windows firewall is NOT running. Now part of this problem may be down to the moronic MSFT Security Console... but some of the blame sits with SYMC. Moronic!
6. The IP scan knocks other PCs off the network. Moronic!

Plus, it slows your PC to a crawl during any activity. So, avoid / avoid / avoid.
top_profane.title: Avoid - Poorly Designed Product

I don't know much about Acronis other than that they are attempting to fill a large gap in the software business at this time, with support for volumes larger than 2TB, which is becoming 'in vogue' with the advent/marketing of large HD's, the likes of which we haven't seen the last of yet. Western Digital, Seagate, and now Hitachi are selling 3TB HD's these days: that is correct in case you didn't know it but in December 2010 all 3 major companies in the hard drive business are selling 3TB HD's! This is cause for great concern amongst the consumer community, because presently there's really no way to use these monster HD's with 3TB of unformatted space, and I'm NOT kidding!

Unless you consider pure, plain, and simple storage as the purpose of having a 3TB volume in your system, that is! Storage, yes it can work...but for an OS to be working on a 3TB GUID volume, or multiple partitions perhaps, nope, cannot be done at this point AFAIK, maybe soon, but no cigar yet for the nice people at Seagate, Hitachi GST, and Western Digital for things 3TB and larger!!

Acronis Disk Director 11 fails MISERABLY at trying to support such HD's, and any volume larger than 2TB for that matter, as this software fails the test of RAID managers and RAID systems overall, and does NOT give the user the tools necessary to utilize 3TB, 4TB, or larger volumes constructed of HD's smaller than 2.2TB, for example a RAID array of 4 x 1TB HD's, especially RAID arrays that are administered by hardware RAID controllers!

The Gosh Danged software doesn't even "See" RAID volumes that are being used by controllers like Areca makes, or Adaptec, any brand of hardware RAID controller for that matter, so the software is totally useless for that sort of application, and it fails miserably in terms of Technical Support from Acronis to boot, and I will tell you all about my experiences with Acronis "24/7 tech support" for this product, which failed just as miserably as the product itself failed my needs.

In short, I wasted almost $50 on this stupid piece of crap software, and I do NOT recommend that anyone here at Amazon.com buy it, for support of volumes, or HD's for that matter, of greater than 2.2TB size, because it will fail miserably, totally, completely to assist the user in administering these large volumes, which Acronis says it was made to do! So go figure that out, nice Amazon.com readers! IT DOESN'T WORK!!!...top put it frankly, mildly, and factually!

I'll repeat it again, THIS SOFTWARE DOES NOT WORK!!!

I bought the software to support an Areca 1680IX-8 Hardware RAID Controller, and there are many others out there that use hardware RAID for administering and managing large volumes of 3TB, 4TB, 5TB, and larger size also, it's not just for Areca controllers that this review is written, it's for ALL RAID CONTROLLERS period! This software cannot SEE 2.2TB or larger GUID Volumes created for hardware, or software RAID for that matter! So what's it worth then to those of us who use these large volumes: NOTHING that is what, it is worth NOTHING AT All!

Buyer Beware here, DO NOT BUY THIS SOFTWARE!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!!

I tried to work with Acronis so-called Tech Support, but they are totally incompetent also! Just like the software is!!! I went through a sordid litany of no less than 11 separate Tech Support Agents at Acronis Software, OUTSOURCED TO INDIA, PAKISTAN, OR SOME OTHER 3rd WORLD COUNTRY I MIGHT ADD, with little to no knowledge about the software, how it works, how it doesn't work, or for that matter how it's supposed to work, and that is putting it MILDLY! These people are pretty stuuuupid, if you ask Wavey Davey!!

top_rant.text:
8/25/2014 - I am going to give Fluenz French 5 stars. I have purchased at least 10 different French programs and must say this is the very best out there. It is clear and right to the point and very easy to understand and navigate throughout. Each lesson starts with a tutorial and then is clearly explained by experienced French teachers and then followed by an array of well done exercises. Sonia Gil is just great!!! You can tell she truly has put her heart and soul into this program. She explains all the lessons in French 1 and 2 and puts everything in an easy and fun way to learn a new language. A true native French teacher explains all the lessons in French 3-5. At present I have only got to lesson 11 in French 1 and I must honestly say I have truly enjoyed it very much and learned a lot. I will write more to my review as I go along so I hope this has helped you make a decision in your purchase. I tell you DON'T hesitate as this is truly the best out there by far and it's no wonder it is in the second position for language learning I am certain it will take the number 1 spot in short order. DON'T THINK ABOUT IT - Buy IT NOW!!! The only negative thing I would say is that the Tu form IS NOT used in what I have learned so far and I know that this form was used by so many each time I visited France. My experience is that most people invite you to use the Tu Form straight away after the initial greeting! This by no means should stop you from purchasing this outstanding program and I am only hoping the tu form will be covered as the program progresses. Enjoy and have fun! Salut!

8/31/14 - Today I finished Fluenz French 1. I can say I have NO regrets to this point. Just about every single question I had after hearing and seeing the tutorials were covered and if not right at the time of any one lesson it was sure to be covered in the very next. At this point still no use of the tu form however, things are changing and being added all the time so I am sure it is on it's way. I can't express enough how great this program is. Does it have any negative parts? Of course but what language program does not? However, I promise they are usually so minor that one can figure it out by themselves and it is no reason to NOT purchase this QUALITY PRODUCT!!! As we have all heard many times in our lives that "Practice Makes Perfect" and "Repetition Is The Mother of Retention" I assure you this program does not fall short of that. Today my wish list would be to see more "Self Talking" drills as they fall short compaired to the other drills in the program,that the lounge music could change for each new set of lessons and that those of you who read the written reviews will NOT judge this program by the few that leave nasty, unpleasant and false information, and that perhaps a written manual could be provided with the program either hardcopy or downloadable form. This program of course needs tweeking but they are on it I assure you of that. So often people purchase a product and find it difficult to grasp and then throw in the towel so to speak. If you are serious about learning a language and willing to spend at least and hour or two everyday I just know you will be speaking the lanquage with a solid base by 6-8 months. That is such a short amount of time to learn the most beautiful language in the world. So I ask you to not listen to others who are negative, put your heart and soul into your studies and you will reap in the rewards for sure.
A note to the staff of the Fluenz team -
"Thank you all so very much for spending countless hours on perfecting this program and my only hope is to see more continued lessons added after Fluenz French 5! I will review again after lesson 15 in Fluenz French 2. Merci Beaucoup!

9/8/2014 - Today I finished with lesson 15 from Fluenz 2 - I realize I am going at a quicker pace than is recommended by the Fluenz staff but I must inform you that I have lots and lots of free time so it makes this possible for me. Anyway, this program just keeps getting better and better. At this point the lessons are getting more challenging and I expect this will continue throughtout. Therefore each lesson is taking me longer to absorb the material. I feel I have a real solid base at this point. By no means am I perfect with everything from all the 45 lessons I have taken so far but I do have a really good understanding of the material. I have decided that when I finish with Fluenz 2 I am going to take the entire levels 1 and 2 again before I move on to Level 3. Like I said before I have tried so many other programs and this one is just brilliant. I'm learning lots of new verbs and building my vocabulary like crazy. I can truly speak sentences and not say just needless stuff like "The ball is yellow or perhaps blue" instead I'm learning to speak everyday conversation and it's a blast. I don't understand the negative comments regarding online help. The staff is always ready to help either by calling or online so those who state otherwise are not correct in saying so. I will update my review after I finish Fluenz Level 2.
`A La Prochaine!

9/16/2014 - Salut! Well I finished Fluenz French 2 today. I am still sticking to my 5 star rating as this is truly a great program. It gets harder as it goes along which is and should be expected. The TU form was finally covered but only for one lesson and that was #19. I understand why it is not being used in this program, however, it does not allow you to keep up the practice when you do meet someone you become very close with and can finally use the informal form. Otherwise it covers many very important grammar points and I have become very fond of using both the French Lounge Music and the Accents Marks features. The music is really soothing while working on complex grammar and why would one not want to learn the correct accent marks right from the beginning??? They are really easy to learn and once you work with them you won't even give it a second thought when they are needed. Well I am off to start Fluenz 3 and will continue my review when I am about half way through. I have decided I would review levels 1 & 2 while working on level 3. As I said I have lots of free time so this has become my new hobby and I love it! Please keep an open mind and DON'T listen to negative feedback about this program. It is truly the best out there!!!

10/12/14 - Bonjour! - 5 S*T*A*R*S* all the way. I just finished lesson 15 in Fluenz 3. This program is just unreal. There is no way one will not learn how to speak french with this outstanding concept. Yes, the lessons are becoming more and more challenging and take longer to complete and the reviews can take even longer as there is so much information to absorb. I have read negative reviews regarding this - WHAT ARE YOU FOLKS THINKING??? - This is the most complete program out there second to NONE!!! I read somewhere "the review lessons are so long and all you do is type, type, type, ya and??? Listen folks, with this program you can't help but learn because of the extensive lessons with every aspect covered - hearing, talking, reading, writing, enjoying and most of all learning and learning again and even again!!! The way it is set up and covers each lesson with so many reviews helps you to retain it without a doubt. I know many who purchase programs and are all into it and everything and as soon as they are challenged they quit!!! Don't get caught up in this and listen to any negative reviews. If you are truly wanting to learn a language then you must understand it, speak it, read it, write it and even THINK in it. With Fluenz you can do all of that. So tell me why you are waiting? GET IT! YOU WILL LEARN TO SPEAK FRENCH!!! I'm beginning to think that all these other programs out there have so many people brainwashed that they will learn to speak a language like magic in an instant. Come on!!! Work for it!!! - the rewards are many!!! I will review again when I finish Fluenz 3. Oh! and yes I am reviewing Fluenz 1 and 2 at the same time and the second time around is so cool to see just how much you really have learned. It's so rewarding to see and hear and UNDERSTAND all aspects. Man I just love this program!!! Salut!

12/4/2014 - WOW!!! That last review for level 3 was INTENSE!, VERY INTENSE!!, VERY VERY INTENSE!!! but I loved it!!! As I said before this program is wonderful! One cannot do anything but learn from it! I see every now and then comments like "It's so repetitive" - "So much tying" - and some other just very stupid complaints! The fact is - if you truly want to speak another language then you have to work for it!!! Plain and Simple! I don't know about you but I was always taught that "REPETITION IS THE MOTHER OF RETENTION" so what are all these comments about how much stuff is repeated? Listen if you want to run with negative people that's your choice but I'd rather stay on the positive side THANK YOU! No one in this program will ever tell you it's gonna be easy! Are there any errors in the program? Of course, but let's be real folks nothing is perfect but I assure you this one is very close to it!!! But, they will say things over and over and over again yes that is true, but guess what? I can speak French!!! Bottom line! I will review again after lesson 15 of level 4. Bonne Chance! Oh, and by the way I now give it 6 *S*T*A*R*S*! Oh and if you see that my reviews are longer in between it's because I am repeating Fluenz 1-3 at the same time I am learning Fluenz 4. I usually do 3 - 4 repeat lessons for every 1 new lessons. It's a great review and so rewarding to see just how much I actually now know. It's so much fun! J'dore Fluenz.

3/11/2015 - Salut tout le monde! I have decided to review after I finished lesson 5 from Fluenz French 4. I had a small change of plans and what I am doing is for every new lesson I learn I am doing 3 or 4 review lessons from previous levels. It had cut down on my working through the new lessons at a faster pace however because the lessons are more and more challanging as they go along and a review lesson is a great treat to be able to more or less breeze through it with a fairly solid understanding and it is truly helping to recall and reimplant anything I had more difficulty understanding or remembering. I have NOT lost my excitement for this wonderful program in anyway if anything each day I actually can't wait to get started with my lessons. I have such a good understanding my new language and for that I am so pleased. I am very proud of my accomplishments but I will tell you that it takes dedication and a true burning desire to want to learn. Which I no doubt have. I igore negative comments from others regarding the program and just keep going and because of it I truly am starting to really speak French. While others are spending their time complaining about small things that can be worked out with ease I choose to truck along in a positive spirit and enjoy all the repitation as it is solidly being reinforced in my subconscious mind. I did want to share that I was introduced to another program called "Ouino" which is another French program. I don't think it is nearly as good as Fluenz however, I do find that it contains lots and lots of vocabulary and again many exercises to work on which will only allow you to build on the foundation that Fluenz has provided. Together I think they work very well and would recommend it to those who are as addicted as I am. Self learning takes lots of dedication and that I have so it's no problem for me. I started Fluenz on 8/25/2014 and my goal is to be taken through the entire program TWICE by 8/25/2015. What is one year of true dedication in your life to learn the most beautiful language in the world. You can do it so get started today and don't wait the clock is ticking. Salut!

8/1/2015 - I have been so very busy that I was not able to ad to my review for quite awhile until today. That being said I am not through the first three level TWICE!!! and I am almost 3/4 way through Fluenz 4. The review lesson 16 for fluenz 4 was very intense and really cover all the material the you learned in this level. I had to slow my studies down because of major surgery but I am back full swing and I will net be able to meet my goal of doing the entire program by 8/25/2015 but because I am so much into this program I am giving myself until 1/31/2016 to do the entire program twice through. I still stay with my 5 star rating and and I promise that if you stick with it you will learn to speak French with out a doubt. I am not sure about the reviews that state that his program is written for the traveler only. That is so far from the truth and if one does feel this way then just put the material you have learned into an everyday situation and bingo you are speaking the language in any standpoint - travel, everyday, present, past, future all of it!!! So stop with the negative and stinking thinking and enjou the wonderful program. Like I said before I have them all and this program really teaches you how to speak the language. Anything we believe we can do and if you believe you can do it you will. I am not the brightest person and never got all A's in school or college. I was a C8 -B student and I tell you I can do this without problem so I know that you can as well. Enjoy and remember - DON'T LISTEN TO THE NEGATIVE REVIEWS AS IF YOU GET CAUGHT UP IN THAT WAY OF THINKING YOU ARE SURE TO FAIL. This program is the best out there by far and the number of 5 star reviews proves it!!! Bonne Chance!!! Salut~

9/25/2015 - Today I finished lesson 25 on Fluenz 4 and tomorrow will start lesson 26 which will be an intense review and I love that because the reviews really make sure that you know and understand the materials that were taught to you earlier. I am very proud to also report to you that I have not finish my second time through Fluenz 1 and 2 and that I am in lesson 11 in Fluenz 3. The second time through made it so much easier to understand everything in a clearer way. I will be working on Fluenz 4 to finish my last 5 lessons on this disk. I will be putting more energies in these last lessons and start my second time through Fluenz 3 with lesson 12 as soon as I am done with all of Fluenz 4. I actually love doing the review of all the lessons I have learned as I have pick up so many small errors I have been making and the review is really clearing things up for me. My French speaking friends are really impressed with my progress and my accent and I can actually hold a conversion with them. Yes, I am making errors along the way but I still have 35 more lessons to finish the whole of the program and I certain I will be able to hold my own with no problem when I am finished. My goal is to finish the entire program a first time and as I am working on the second time through while doing so and after I am finish with going through for the second time. I will be taking a break for about a week or so and then I am Going to wiz through the entire program for a third time from start to finish. I am certain when I am done I will speak French well. I encourage you to read and listen to French TV and movies when you can to build your ear for the language and speak, speak, speak, French all the time even if it's to your self. You will be amazed at how much you really know. I love this program and I pay no attention to the reviewers who down it. This is the best out there and that is all there is to it so why fool yourself with others programs that just don't work. Remember "Repetition is the mother of retention" and you surely get that with Fluenz French without doubt. Salut et Bonne Chance!

4/16/16 - I know - why such a long delay if you really love this program? Here is the deal. I wanted to learn the French language so bad that I would allow NOTHING to get in my way and I purchase just about all the major French programs out there. I decided I would take each one of the French courses I purchased and finish them in the order I purchased them. So for the last year I have now FINISHED the whole of Ouino, Pimsleur and Rosetta Stone. And now I am back to finish my Fluenz program. Actually I have finished lessons 26 - 29 in Fluenz 4 and today starting the last lesson #30 in that level and then onto Fluenz 5. I STILL AM HOLDING FAST TO MY OPINION THAT FLUENZ FRENCH HAS TAUGHT ME THE MOST IN THE FASTED WAY POSSIBLE. Today I actually can speak the language quit well for on and off studying over the last 2 years. What I have only half learned with the other programs Fluenz has made very clear so I can truly understand what is being taught. Yes - I do agree that the program is based on mostly travel however, all the lessons can be used in any situation they just happened to have picked travel as the setting. If you open your mind and don't let the negative comments of others get in the way I am certain you will learn the very best studying with Fluenz French. Get it you will love it!!! So? What are you waiting for?

9/1/16 - Today I finished the last lesson of Fluenz 5 - I went through the entire program and for that I am most proud. And guess what?
I truly speak the language very well. Fluenz 5 was not an easy task. It took long hours and lots of study for me to finish each lesson but in the end I was able to absorb all the material with very little not understood. I actually worked the very hardest on Fluenz 5 because I was away from it for awhile as I explained in earlier writings and I wanted to make up for that so full speed ahead I went and finished all of Fluenz 5 finishing up the very last lesson on 8/31/16. The is so much information in my head that all I do is think about and speak French. And quess what I am now doing the entire program over again. I will post on this as I am going along. PLEASE DON'T THINK ABOUT IT ANYMORE - PURCHASE THIS PROGRAM IT'S IS TRULY A JOY TO WORK WITH AND YOU ARE SURE TO LEARN TO SPEAK THE LANGUAGE YOU CHOOSE. GOOD LUCK!

9/11/16 - Just a very quick post to let know that I have now finished the entire Fluenz 1 for the third time and even after 3 times through I picked up a few things I was not sure about but I now know the material cold. My plan is to finish the entire program yet again.
As of today I finished the first 6 lessons in Fluenz 2 and again picking up a few things so far that I had difficulties with but now understand fully and I expect that will be the case throughout the remainer of the lessons to the very end. As for myself I have always been one of those people that believes in doing more difficult things a minimum of three times to my goal is the have finished the entire course of Fluenz 1-5 three times fully through. If I can do it so can you and I promise you will learn to speak the beautiful French language.
I will keep posting as time go along. Now I wish and hope that Fluenz French will come out with a Fluenz 6 as I will be all over that.
Best to you all!

UPDATE: In response to commenters doubting the accuracy of my original review, I REWROTE my review to clarify my reasons for asserting that REAL ILLUSION IS CHEATING CUSTOMERS BY SELLING PATCHED OLD TECHNOLOGY, as follows:

iClone 5 requires Windows XP's DirectX 9 in order to work in Windows 7. DirectX 11 is Windows 7 technology.

Reallusion didn't bother to write new code to make iClone 5 work with W7's DirectX 11. XP's DirectX 9 components are installed during installation of iClone. That means instead of writing new code for W7, Reallusion used a cheap 'workaround' to get iClone 5 to function 'OK' in W7. That method of developing a 'new' version prevents iClone 5 from unleashing and using the full power of W7 technology. Yes, iClone 5 works OK in W7 but at the expense of performance...thus shortchanging iC5 users.

Run iClone 5 in XP SP3 x32 and compare its performance against iClone 5 in W7 x64...you will notice iClone runs faster and smoother in XP. (I, myself, use iClone 5 in XP rather than in Windows 7. I have a dual OS Windows PC--both XP & W7. I would gladly run iClone 5 in W7 if it performed better in W7.)

Reallusion's patch job might work OK in W7 x64. BUT for $180 to $200, Real Illusion should have re-written iClone for W7 technology, not patching XP technology to work in W7. A patch job is dirty code; and clean code works more efficiently than 'dirty' patched code...ask any software programmer! Real Illusion took the cheapo developer's path at the expense of their customers.

Reallusion didn't officially make iClone 5 compatible with W7 64-bit until late June 2012 with release of v5.3--OVER two years after the release of W7! AGAIN, instead of writing new code, Real Illusion just patched their old technology to function OK in a 64-bit OS. I can cite other instances of programming shortcuts taken by Real Illusion to transform XP's iClone 4 into the bastardized version called iClone 5.

What happens now in Windows 8? Will XP's DirectX 9 work in Windows 8? If not, forget using iClone 5 in Windows 8. But if Reallusion had written iC5's code for W7, then iC5 would already be compatible with W8.

Hey, if you don't mind buying yesterday's technology for big bucks, more power to you. But I am a consumer who feels that for $180, I should get a truly new product rather than an old product with a new facelift.

*** ORIGINAL REVIEW ****

The DIRTY TRUTH is Reallusion could have developed iClone 5 for Windows 7 x64 but did not!

In Reality, Reallusion patched up iClone 4 for Windows 7. Then Reallusion renamed version 4, calling it iClone 5!

In Other Words, iClone 5 is not actually a major new version created specifically for Windows 7. iClone was created for Windows XP and then PATCHED for Windows 7. And they did a poor patch job too!

Clone 5 Pro and Windows 7 SP1 x64 are NOT Friends!

iClone 5 Pro requires the user to install Windows XP's DirectX 9. If you don't, iClone will NOT work.

DirectX 9 was the version for Windows XP. DirectX 11 is Windows 7's version. And iClone REQUIRES installation of DirectX 9 in Windows 7! (I wonder how iClone will work in Windows 8!)

Microsoft claims DirectX 11 is backwards compatible for OLD games and OLD software that require DirectX 9. Microsoft even recommends against installing DirectX 9 in Windows 7 with DirectX 11. Microsoft DownloadS' web installer refuses to install DirectX 9 in Windows 7. (It is possible to install DirectX 9 in Windows 7 but only if you manage to find a 'redistributable' package.)

As a general rule, it is a bad idea to install old operating system components into a newer operating system. Yet, that is exactly what Reallusion wants you to do.

Under the hood, iClone 5 is nothing more than iClone 4 with additional features. iClone 4 was developed for Windows XP technology; therefore, iClone 5 fundamentally remains XP era software. Even Reallusion more or less admits such!

The Reallusion senior technician wrote this: "We can totally understand your frustration in using iClone5 on Windows 7. And actually our development team also considers upgrading DirectX to newer version. But because of many reasons, we didn't do this update in iClone5. However, they still consider to update it in iClone 6."

iClone 5 was released almost two years after the release of W7. Reallusion had ample time to properly update iClone 5 for DirectX 11 of W7. Instead they chose to patch iClone 4 to sort of work with W7 x64. (iClone 4 was developed for Windows XP and DirectX 9.) The patch job called iClone 5 does not take full advantage of W7's power and capabilities.

iClone's performance in Windows 7 suffers as a consequence of using OLD technology. That fact can be seen in the poor quality of iClone's output.

iClone 5 can do amazing things with animation, but the animation output looks like something from the 1990s.

Animated people do not look realistic. They look like mannequins in a clothing store. Their facial expressions are blank and dead. But what can be expected when Reallusion iClone 5 continues to rely on OLD technology to produce animation?

iClone 5 has Bugs in Windows 7 x64

Reallusion did a lousy patch job to make iClone work in Windows 7 x64. Consequently, iClone 5 suffers from several significant 'bugs' in W7 x64.

First of all, Reallusion does not warn you in advance that it must install DirectX during the iClone installation!

DURING installation of iClone, a message pops up and tells the user that he must install DirectX 9 in his W7 PC--that is a very rude surprise!

My first and second installations of iClone 5 Pro failed on account of issues with DirectX 9.

Yes, I managed to get iClone 5 Pro to finally install in W7 SP1 x64. BUT it is a patch job¡­ and as such, iClone works like crap now.

iClone 5 Pro fully opens up OUTSIDE of the right edge of my monitor screen. That means the Minimize & Exit buttons in the upper right corner can't be accessed.

The close button of the iClone icon on Taskbar doesn't work. Also, the 'close' item on the dropdown menu in the upper left corner under the Reallusion icon does not work.

It is tough closing out iClone. I have to 'force close' it using Windows Task Manager. That really sucks!

Some features in menus won't launch by mouse click, ­but will open if the correct hot key is used. That sucks too.

I can name other issues to, ­but there is also a bitter irony involved too.

Since iClone didn't perform so well in W7, I installed it on XP. My PC is dual OS--XP Pro SP3 x32 and W7 Pro SP1 x64.

Ha, ha, iClone 5 Pro works like a champ in XP x32 (if you don't mind using a nearly obsolete OS and getting limited to 3 GB of RAM).

Bad performance in W7 but good performance in XP should tell you something about iClone.

OVERALL, I am unhappy with my purchase of iClone 5 Pro. I didn't want to use it in XP!

Reallusion Does Not Update Its Other Software Too!

The same lack of proper development can be seen in OTHER Reallusion software.

I cite WidgetCast Pro, which is a companion software for iClone. The last update for Widget was released in 2010.

WidgetCast's tech specs warn customers that not all features work in Windows 7.

WORSE YET, Reallusion is a liar!

Reallusion continues to aggressively market WidgetCast even though they stopped updating it in 2010. EVEN MORE HORRIBLE, Reallusion boldly advertises WidgetCast as compatible with Windows 7...even when Reallusion's own spec sheet says otherwise.

Oh yes, I bought WidgetCast too and discovered the hard way that it doesn't work in W7 x64! But all features work in Windows XP!

It wasn't until current version 5.3 that iClone was officially made compatible with W7 64-bit--and v5.3 was just released within the past month or so! Unfortunately, the latest version still doesn't work quite right in Windows 7 x64 (see above for my report about bugs in iClone 5.3).

The REAL ILLUSION is Reallusion's claim that iClone works well in Windows 7 x64! And the Real Lie is the claim that iClone 5 was a NEW major version!

Reallusion is a lazy, lying developer--and that is the Real Truth behind the Real Illusion!

POOR TECH SUPPORT

Reallusion tech support is a Real Illusion. They are quick to offer excuses. But they don't provide meaningful support that actually fixes anything.

This summarizes Real Illusion's concept of tech support: 'Our software is perfect. But you are not! And that's the Real problem!

REPEAT -- Remember This Before You Buy iClone 5:

1. Reallusion did NOT fully update iClone for Windows 7. Reallusion did a lousy patch job to make iClone work in Windows 7 x64. And iClone's performance in Windows 7 is poor. And thus its animation output look badly outdated.

2. iClone employs OLD XP technology! Don't buy old technology!

REVIEW SMURF VILLAGE
The Smurf Village game is about recreating the village after Gargamel destroyed it.
It's cute yes, (Smurf's are always cute) and the game was fun at the beginning, but became tiresome leading to the point of dead boring!!
Any games you play just can't be all about being "cute". Yes it was fun at the beginning but that wore off in time after realizing what the game was doing to me, I've stopped spending money on this game because it was sucking me dry financially! A game is only fun if it offers you so much more for your money which this game doesn't. This game will suck you dry financially if you let it. I would not recommend this game for kids or even adults personally because:
1) compared to 2010, Smurf Village is NOW full of adverts to entice you with other games available on the app that Beeline developed to make even more money or shall I say, suck it from you if you allow it!. These days with the adverts on full throttle, I found rather frustrating when you first turn on your iPad to press "start" and end up being brought to the app store immediately. To prevent this annoying regular occurrence from happening, you have to wait a few seconds to allow the screen for infomercials and ads to pop up first, just in case there is any before pressing the "start" button. I've already paid at least $40 or more and still continually bombarded with adverts where other apps request only .99 cents to remove adverts! Come on!! (Many years ago, apparently there was heavy advertising going on through Twitter where in order to get Smurf berries for free you have to go to Twitter to promote Smurf Village. Eventually Twitter got tired of a number of ppl doing this and some ppl even got booted off Twitter. This happened during the time Smurf Village was giving away free smurf berries during Valentine season. There is no such thing as free, there's always a catch!!)
2) It takes forever to build the village. For eg, I have in my Space (Swoof) planet: 7 trees each gives 500XP; Baker's hut plus 2 accessories beside him gives 700XP and bought with gold coins (from Smurfs planting vegetables in the garden) items that give 350XP; and so I have bought about 2000 of these items; (2000 items x 350XP = 700,000XP in total and all together 700,000XP + 500XP + 700XP = 16% increase of leveling up! (Talk about rip-off! You would think with that kind of amount of XP accumulated, you would get ahead a lot but NO!)To level up quicker, you have to work extremely hard by planting, buy lots of items that will give lots of XP and playing the mini-games provided by Handy Smurf's, Papa Smurf, Miner Smurf, Baker Smurf etc. All this takes up so much of your time. By the way, most of these mini-games will make you WAIT FROM 5-24hrs before you can play again. The amount of "XP" given after playing the mini-games pending which one's you play can range from 100-1200XP. Even playing these mini-games will not get you leveled up much. You really have to SPEND REAL MONEY to help you a bit.
3) The worst part about spending money on this game is this: they will charge you $9.99 for a 125 berries. 125 berries may seem a lot but its not! Why? Because each accessories or items that helps generate berries will cost you 25-35 berries you just bought and more depending what you want. 125 berries will not get you very far which means you'll have to spend more real money!! Another thing is once you get the items for eg the Windmill which generates ONE berry once a week, you will have to rebuild it after purchasing it with Smurfberries. Some of the items you bought with your berries will require up level 5 or 8 stages to build. To build it in stages you will be required more rocks, wood, dyes and stardust, which means more work for you to generate rocks, wood, etc. This also takes forever it would seem because for eg, you only get 9 rocks from Miner Smurf; 12 wood from Timber Smurf on each play and depending which mini games you play can take you up to 24 hours of wait before you can play the mini games again. On top of this, the asking amount of rocks, wood, dyes etc., from the stages ranges from 75-800 for each items per level stages. For example stage 1 of an item may require 250 rocks, 455 wood etc. Remember, your mini-games provided only allows you to play at a limited time plus the resources it gives out is not enough to quickly build your item like the Windmill for eg.
Before you start spending $9.99 to $99.00 + on this game, think wisely what your money can buy versus just a game that doesn't even stimulate your child's mind because all you do is press buttons on this game, there is no mental challenge offered in the Smurf Village. With the price you pay for the Smurf Village, you're better off with games like Minecraft for eg, that allows children to develop their creativity further because you actually have to gather the items and make them, (in Smurf Village, its already made for you you just have to use berries a lot, spend a lot of money to create your village. Real creativity is putting ideas together from scratch and turning it into something unique which is what Minecraft allows for in children but not Smurf Village however!) Another game I would highly recommend is Eufloria. Both Minecraft and Eufloria are available on the iPad/iPhone and is not an "in-app purchase" which means you only pay once and that's it. The asking price is also so much less than Smurf Village!!!!!
4) its very addictive and if you have a tendency for a gambling habit, a spendthrift, or just plain impatient, games like the Smurf Village will have you spending your money in no time at all, so be very careful. So spend with caution and discipline yourself financially if you are going to download this game! There's so many other useful and good things to buy on Amazon for $9.99-200.00.
5) When you plant your vegetables and forget to harvest, it will wilt, I repeat, it will WILT, does Farmville and other games do this to their players? I don't know!! However with Smurf Village, there is no mercy!! I do believe this game take so much from consumers and give very little in return!! If you call spending fun because this is what the game is aimed at continually, then you must be a spendthrift or someone who doesn't value hard earned cash. Don't even worry about going VIP because there is no real privilege there either!! In the end you end up spending more real berries even after you've already spent $200 worth of berries with them to get to the VIP level. What VIP does offer is that it entices you to buy unique items NOT available to non VIP members. Really, wow, what a bonus, all that $200 worth of accumulated spending and all you get is unique items which means spend more REAL HARD EARNED MONEY!!? Lol, unfortunately, so many people already spent this kind of money. I'm not a VIP but was almost tempted so I took the time to research and google info's on peoples complaints and comments from other websites about being a VIP vs non VIP members. VIP eh?! It's only a name status and don't mean a thing. VIP Status is nothing if you don't have nothing to show for in real life because you're too busy spending it on useless things like Smurf Village games!

Anyways, I can't tell you what to do, I can only go by my experience so if this is what you want and don't mind spending your hard earned REAL money then, go for it! Just remember people get poorer because they spend frivolously on useless things that have no value or monetary return for what they spent on. ((There's so many useful things on Amazon and Smurf Village isn't one of them!))
I can't understand why this particular app gets a four star from so many ppl. There must be a lot of people out there who likes to spend frivolously or don't appreciate the value of money!! There are so many apps that help develop the mind and are so much more fun to play without spending heavily, but this game is not one of them!! Smurf Village is the most frustrating and consumer unfair game I have ever played!! Sadly, there are other game apps now following this suit of greediness. top_rant.text:
I'm another long time user from JASC days. I already bought and installed this in the original version then bought the upgraded version. I also bought a couple books that were written about it, this version specifically, because I'm not short sighted enough to claim to know how to do everything and my memory is somewhat porous these days and forgetting things is a way of life.

First off, I installed this on a new PC using the latest Intel Core i7 980X 3.3 GHz 6 core processor, 24 GB DDR3 RAM, Windows 7 Ultimate 64 bit. I have 2 eVGA GeForce GTX 580 Black Ops video cards with 1536 MB memory each operating in SLI mode. A fact is that graphic processing is a very demanding process.

Here are some complaints I'd like to offer an opinion on:

I see many people panning this program for what they describe as slow operation, over the top memory and resource use, etc. This isn't necessarily a software issue. I installed the basic version of this program on my older PC which has a Pentium 4 @ 2.2 GHz and 2 GB RAM running XP Pro SP3. It ran OK but nothing like on the new PC. In one forum someone said people having problems need to install a bigger hard drive along with the "they are cheap and easy to put in" lecture. Trust me, that will NOT make things run faster unless the installed drive is chock full. A bigger drive is fine, but as cheap as external USB drives are these days, no need to open the computer case unless it's necessary. (I just bought a 2 TB USB/eSATA external drive for $89.00.) The bottom line is maybe your PC is no longer up to the task. An always good fix for slow PCs is add RAM! A great site that will analyze your PC and walk you through everything you need to do and then tell you exactly what you need is Crucial.com. But be aware, especially with Windows 7 all 32 bit versions of the program are limited in how much RAM they can use! Even if you have the room and can buy the modules, the OS (operating system) will not "see" them! Different versions of the Windows 7 64 bit OS have RAM limits as well. Ultimate and Professional are way out there with how much RAM they can use, something like 104 GB. Your PC may run out of slots first! Another thing is even if you have a lot of RAM, if you have a bunch of programs running in the background like virus detection, TiVo server, file monitors and all the other stuff you see in Task Manager (ctrl, alt, and del)ALL are running in RAM. If you have 2 GB RAM and 60% is running back ground tasks you actually have under 1 GB for programs to use. With XP those are all those icons on the left by the clock. If you have several dozen of them, guess what?

Nobody reads them but it is always important to read the tiny little print on software packages or in product descriptions called "Minimum System Requirements". These lay out what the software needs at a minimum to run. If you are at or close to minimum requirements do not expect the software to run right. Oh, it may run, but I promise you will have problems! I am also a PC gamer and I know too well of how being at these minimums means things will not work as you expect or want. "Minimum System Requirements" translates to "This program will install and may actually start! But, it's just a ruse! It will stutter, stall, freeze, hang, crash, and demonstrate every bad behavior known to PCs. Closely meeting these minimum requirements is guaranteed to make you angry and at some point you will entertain notions of throwing your computer out of the window!" The more you exceed the minimums, the better off you will be and the better your PC will run the program.

Some installation and operational problems I have seen with other software using Windows 7 are corrected by doing an "Install as Administrator". I have no interest in providing a Windows guide or tech explanation of this. Just know with Windows 7 if you have trouble installing a program, try installing as an administrator. If a program doesn't run right, uninstall it and reinstall as an administrator. Just because your account type says you are an administrator there are still restrictions and manually telling Windows what to do is necessary. Open the CD/DVD or installation file, find the install icon, right click it and there in that menu is the "Install as Administrator" command. Click it and go. I believe there is also a "Run as Administrator" but I have not used it so I can't comment on it. The whole double administrator thing is a safety thing with Windows 7 because people can get too complacent installing things and even though they may have administrator privelidges, there are times when they need something to make them think about what they are installing. I strongly recommend "The Missing Manual" if you are using Windows 7 because it will explain the ins and outs of Administrator accounts and is a superb, plain language manual indispensable to anyone using Windows 7 regardless of their PC OS savviness. I have probably a dozen problem programs I had difficulty with that either worked fine after re-installing as Administrator or installing them that way after repeated installation failures. I found recommendations online for some programs suggesting they be installed that way from the get-go. I think it bears repeating- educate yourself on Windows 7 and Administrator accounts.

I have read this version of PSP X3 is incompatible with 64 bit Windows 7. I am running it on Windows 7 Ultimate 64 bit and have had no crashes, freezes, lock ups, slow downs, etc. or any bad behavior.

I see people saying to go to Adobe Photoshop. I tried Photoshop Elements and it was bare bones compared to PSP. For what I do It wasn't an acceptable alternative and it cost the same and usually more. The full on Adobe Photoshop is still far too expensive for me. A quick price check showed it ranging from the high $400 range to $600. My thinking is for that money upgrade your computer or put it towards a new one. If you shop online there are some deals to be had. A newer better PC will have you thanking yourself. If the problem is an underpowered PC on your part installing a pricey and also demanding program isn't going to help. If anything, it may be worse.

Please read my update at the bottom regarding this. I'm going to leave this in even though I solved the problem. The only thing I don't like about the new PSP is not the fault of PSP as far as I can tell. I retired my old TWAIN scanner and bought a new photo scanner. PSP will not see it. I'm still working on a resolution to that. For now I have to scan into a file using the scanner mfg's software, and then I have to access that folder from PSP. Not a big deal really, but I did become used to scanning from within PSP. There should be a way to get it to work with WIA, but I'm still working on that. That is my only reason for not giving PSP 5 stars. Corel should have understood people like to use one program for interfacing all their hardware into and accommodated them.

Lastly, there are comments that there isn't much different between this X3 version and the last few previous versions. All I can say is that's relative. Sometimes the improvements are made to parts of the program those with that complaint don't use. What's been changed is quite clearly listed and written about on the web site. If you read it and don't see that any of the enhancements and/or changes apply to how you use the software, don't buy the new one! It sounds like some people bought the new version just because it was new and didn't take the time to see if it was worth buying. When no obvious changes were apparent they blamed the software rather than themselves for not researching it first. New does not always mean everything is better or everything that was changed is absolutely necessary in every case. Almost all software is packed with features that most users will never bother with. I can't answer what expectations people had in their minds in the rush to upgrade. It seems to me they get the new version and complain because it doesn't seem immediately new but few really say what it is they are specifically unhappy about with the software itself. As I said already, what they complain about sounds more like hardware problems than software. I bought it because the changes directly affected what I do with the software. I went to Corel's site. I looked. I read. I compared. I shopped for the best price. It's all clearly explained. People forget that not every software improvement is out front and mind bending mind-candy! It may be in the coding or adding new features that some users wanted but others don't need. The complainers remind me of the kids in school who never studied or did homework and then blamed the teachers and the school for their failures. Simple logic says if this product was so bad, so failure prone, and so useless there wouldn't be a market for it. car companies can get away with wrapping the same old stuff in a new package and pass it off as new year after year because appearance is all most look at. With software it's performance and features that matter- not pretty graphics and unusual fonts.

The craziest comments were saying Corel was intentionally making PSP worse so people would stop buying it! That is just so ridiculous! What software company would be so ignorant as to intentionally sully their reputation by intentionally releasing crap and making it worse every year? It's a fact that consumers always blame the company, not just the product, when bad stuff is sold to people. Believe me, if Corel wanted to stop selling PSP, they would just stop selling it and drop it from their catalog. Think again with just a grain of reason and logic- what has really progressed in computing every year? Computers and their processing power! What is the most typical improvement made to all kinds of software? Making use of the increased computer power, increased memory capacity, faster peripherals! What is the most likely thin in the PC/software partnership that will struggle? The less powerful, older machines! Even a few year old PC may suffer trying to run newer software. It happens all the time! Just because you paid a lot of money for your computer 5 years ago and it was damn good in its day doesn't mean it's still top notch today! As a gamer I have suffered through this technical obsolescence time and again over the years! Until I got my new PC I had games that I couldn't even install and others that installed but ran pathetically slow so much they were unplayable. It certainly wasn't the games themselves that were causing the problems. It was a PC that didn't have what it took to run them. And the demand for a more powerful PC has continued to increase. Ask any gamer about the demand of the original Crysis game released in 2007. *0% of PCs when it was new could only run it on extremely reduced settings if it could run at all. Even today it is still a PC humbler. The moral: don't blame the software if you have an economy or old PC and don't know how it stacks against what is needed to run your software.

The people who complain about problems running this program don't share their hardware information. Why? Because they don't understand the relationship between the two. They never checked to see what is needed and it's doubtful most know what they have. I have worked with computers and electronics for 30+ years so I understand many of the problems and can opine on the cause. Some may be trying to run it on a Pentium 3 with 256 MB of RAM and wondering why it won't run right!

Back to what I said about books in regards to explanations and instructions on how to use the software: invest in some! You can never have too much information! The two books I bought together with the small printed manual that came with the upgraded version of PSP X3 contain over 1000 pages of information! While I may not use everything this software can do or use features all the time, I am not foolish enough to believe I "know" everything and am certainly not capable of remembering everything. There again, I question if some actually know the right way to do the things they have trouble with. Drawing on my experience as an electronics and computer tech I can assure people reading this that 90% of problems with computer software based anything are caused by those at the keyboards. Assembly instructions for book cases and baby strollers aren't the only user instructions people like to ignore!

Here's a bit of what I do with my software to put in perspective how I use PSP:

I am restoring old family photos some of which are in bad shape. I scan them, fix them and the go onto disk which get distributed to family members.
I build scale military miniatures. I photography them with a green screen background and then insert the images is various backgrounds to create period correct military photos.
I take photos of my friends and do embarrassing things to them.
I make special effect artsy photos.
I photograph pictures in old books, especially historical photos of military equipment and add them to my private reference archives.
I process pictures for eBay auctions.
I work on photos for friends both fixing still shots. I use still shots and video clips with other software to create DVDs for friends. A favorite is DVDs of their kids participating in school sports activities.
I've processed photographs to use in slide show type DVD presentations for birthdays, anniversaries, weddings, car shows, motorcycle shows, air shows, and other events.
I edit, repair, print, and save to disc my own photographs.
I just fool around with taking pictures, doing things to the pictures both for fun and to practice with and try new things with the software to get a handle on everything that can be done with it.

So, having first bought the plain version of PSP X3 I liked it so well I bought the upgraded version with the bonus software, sold the plain version I had bought, and got a friend to buy a standard version for his projects. I think it's that good I don't hesitate to recommend it.

To recap, I have not had ANY of the problems that others have complained about. Having bought PSP X3 and tried it I would buy it again.

UPDATE: I found out that to associate my scanner, an Epson Perfection V600, with PSP Photo X3 running under Windows 7 Ultimate 64 bit I had to do the following: Open Devices and Printers from Control Panel. Right click on the scanner icon. from the menu that opens click on Scan Properties. Next comes the verification window asking for permission to allow the program to make changes to the computer. Click Yes. In the next window that opens click the tab labeled Events. Look to the box at the bottom labeled Action. Select the Start This Program button. (It may already be selected)From the drop down menu to the right of that select Corel Paintshop Photo Pro X3.

After doing all that I can open PSP and the scanner functions under File > Import are no longer grayed out and I can scan directly into PSP. When I press the scan button on the scanner PSP opens and the image is scanned and displayed in it.

Of course, if you use other programs with your scanner one of the selections is to ask which program you want to open with the scanner or you can select a program other than PSP. I prefer PSP as my default image processing program so my setting are perfect for me.

If you have a different scanner I would assume the process is similar and what I described should get you there or awfully close. I should point out that my new scanner does not use a TWAIN driver. So much for another complaint that you can't tie a non-TWAIN scanner in with PSP X3 under Windows 7!
